ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: Nwagbe ɔɖuɖu ɔba sɔ si nnɛ i Ɣaa ɔɣɛ ɔkigu i ɔ̃ kanyaɖidze ɔwɛ̃ iso aaba i ne ame sɔ,
translated: When Nwagbe finished eating, he went outside and saw that someone was coming towards him.

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Gɔ Herodes ɔkpi ne, Ɣaa kpabo ɔwɛ̃ ɔɖi so ɔte Yosef i kurɛ ame i Egipte.
translated: After Herod died, an angel appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.

ref: Joseph got up and left with them for Israel.
text: Ne ɔso Yosef ɔta ɔkɔ ɔbi gu ɔnyi marui makɛlɛ Israel karɔ̃ iso.
translated: Joseph got up, took the child and his mother, and went to the land of Israel.

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.
text: Mite i mi nsɛgbai ame sɔ miɔfiniki mibɔrɛ i mi akpi ame.
translated: If you see a stranger greet them and give them food.

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Awu wa i Yohanes ɔpia ne, kapuipɔngɔ siwɛrɛ̃ masu mawe ala, fiɛ ɔsu bɔi ɔko kafiami ɔtɔ so ɔsɔ. Maɣendu gu kugbadzedzei loɖe ɔ̃ aɖera.
translated: John’s father said to him, “Take this basket and go to the farm. When you get there, collect some yams and bring them home.”

ref: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
text: Ɔ̃iwe i Nasaret, ɣɛɛ ɔkɛlɛ Kapernaum, ɔmagɛ̃ gɔ nse i Galilea itubu kanya i Zebulon gu Naftali karɔ̃ iso.
translated: When he left Nazareth, he went and lived in Capernaum, which is by the lake in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali in Galilee.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Mmɔ i Tsirise ɔba ɔ̃ kɔrɛ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Si fɔ nɖe Ɣaa Ɔbi ne, ɣere ata wagbe sɔ akpese aɖera.”
translated: When Tsirise’s child returned home, he said, “If you go to Ɣaa Ɔbi’s place, you will eat delicious food every day.”

ref: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
text: “Mi nse lɛ mma mitã maturi ɔɖuɖu, ɣɛɛ si mma ɔyu me ɔmɛrɛ̃ ne, maisibawo ɔri kuwɛ̃ iso ɔki ɔpia me ɔmɛrɛ̃. Misiina kusia kukuwɛ̃ iɖo sɔ maasu me mawiri maturi ɔsɛ me iso.
translated: We do not eat the young maize cobs, but when the maize is ripe, women roast it on the fire and eat it. Sometimes, they also cook it in the pot together with beans and eat it.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: “Ɔwiɔ ne minɔ masɛ maɣɛ ana sɔ, ‘Daatu ndamu, Ɣɛɛ bara i nnɛ aɣɛ sɔ aabara atã Ɣaa iso.’
translated: The chief told me, ‘Don’t worry, come here tomorrow and I will give you three yams.’

ref: You know you have been taught, “An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.”
text: “Minɔ masɛ maɣɛ sɔ, ‘Si abiɛ fɔ ɔlaa inɔmi ne, maabiɛ fɔ ire, fiɛ si akpa fɔ ɔlaa inyɛ ne, maakpa fɔ ire fɔ wũ.’
translated: My father told me, ‘If you take something that is not yours, you have taken trouble; but if you take what is yours, you have taken peace.’

ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Si misɛ miɖɔɛ mma nsɛ maɖɔɛ mi ɔnɔwɛ̃ ne, be kuso miafɔ i Ɣaa kɔrɛ? Ma lampofɔdze ma wũ masɛ mabara ngbɔ.
translated: If I greet you and you do not greet me back, how will our friendship grow? That is why I always try to be polite.

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: “Fiɛ si mito mikparama kayi ne, midaase lɛ mma nsɛ mabara ara si mabo maturi anɔ. Masɛ mabie sɔ maata maya makparama kayi i kasɔrɛkɔ̃ gu kuɣa ame sɔ maturi si manya ma. Lobie sɔ lotã mi itsue sɔ ma kuso ame maɔfɔ mmɔ ne.
translated: When I take my bag in the morning, I thank my mother because she bought the cloth that I used to sew the bag. I know that my father also gave me money to buy the thread that I used. My siblings are happy when they see me going to school with my new bag.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: Ɣɛɛ fɔ si ato akparama kayi ne, kɛlɛ fɔ iyo kame si asɛ̃ iyo apia so fiɛ aakpere fɔ Ɔse gɔ maisɛ manya ala anɔmi, iyɔ gɔ ɔ̃ ɔsɛ ɔnya ta awɛrɛra ne, ɔto ɔ̃atã-ɔ kuso.
translated: If someone wants to buy a sheep, they must first find out from the owner whether the sheep is for sale, because if not, they may get into trouble.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: Fɔ sigara iɖe si siba i bo ndɛ̃. Maabara fɔ kuɖɔɛ i kayiiso lɛ kumɛgɔ masɛ mabara i kato.
translated: If you smoke a lot, you will get sick. That is why the elders always advise us not to smoke.

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: Ne loatã sɔ maturi iibatsue sɔ ato anyi kanya, ɣɛɛ fɔ Ɔse gɔ maisɛ manya, fiɛ ɔsɛ ɔnya ara wɛrɛa aatã-ɔ kuso. Kato Nɖe Ara Ka Kɔlakɔ̃ Sɛɛ.
translated: If you want to harvest yams early in the morning, you must first ask the owner, so that he will not be angry with you. This is the way things are done.

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: Ɣɛɛ nnɛ iso i mi anɔ si arɛtɛ nɖe sɔ Ɣaa si ɔɖe sigara i mi iso gu si mibara sibɔlɔlɔ ara iyɔ ɔto ɔ̃asu ara wagbe ɔɖuɖu ɔtã mi.
translated: But today I want to tell you about Gaa who used to smoke cigarettes but stopped because he realized it was not good for his health.

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: “Be ɔso mito mimararã so i nnɛ miapia iso? Minyɔ ipo ame adziri kumɛgɔ asɛ amɔ. Aisɛ ata ɖɔti ɣee awe sikati.
translated: If I go to the farm today, will you come with me? There are many people working there now. But the ground is wet because it rained last night.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Ɣee ɔsu aɣɛ ɔtã ɔ̃ ɔbi si ɔkarɛ wũ ikpɛ?
translated: Who will take care of the child if the mother is not around?

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
text: Kado ɔpɛ, siwore ɔyi, awɛ ɔkpɛ̃ teteree ɣɛɛ iyo iirɔ̃ ala mapia ne karɔ̃ ame kukaakɔ.
translated: When the rain falls, the river rises, and the frogs come out to sing and play with their friends.

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
text: Mmaratedze ɔwɛ̃ ɔba ɔ̃atu wũ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Ratedze, loasiai-ɔ kakɔi biara ga aakɛlɛ.”
translated: The child’s mother called her and said, “Child, don’t go anywhere without telling me.”

ref: a terrible storm suddenly struck the lake, and waves started splashing into their boat. Jesus was sound asleep,
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ ne, awɛ siare awɛ̃ ɔtsɛ ikpɛ̃ i itubu iso gɔ itã ndu to mita mibo i ɔkolo ame, ɣɛɛ Yesu rɛ ɔto ɔrɛ sirɛ.
translated: Long ago, when people were still living in darkness and did not know the way to God, God sent his son, Jesus, to show them the way.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Makparako ikuri iwɛ̃ ɣɛ i ndɛ̃ mɛmɔ isɛ mato maɖe ara.
translated: The hunter said he would not go to the farm again.

ref: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔbo iyo ame ne, maba ɔ̃ kɔrɛ, ne ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Mifɔ miɖe sɔ loawo mi ɔsa?” Matã mmuai sɔ, “Aĩ, Bosate!”
translated: When Jesus saw the man, he called him and asked, “Do you want to be healed?” The sick man replied, “Yes, Lord!”

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
text: Ne Ɔso ɔɣere ɔ̃ marasuãdze sɔ, “Ɔraɖui gɔ maasɔrɛ̃ ɔsi, ɣɛɛ marabaradze iisi.
translated: The woman said to her husband, "Go and bring firewood, I will prepare food."

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: “Ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ne, maturi to maaɖi ma manyii matã sɔ maɖoe fiɛ mase ana to maaɖi ma mabi matã. Mabi to maata maya ma maɣese iso maɖi ma matã maɖoe.
translated: When someone is angry, their heart is not calm and their words are not sweet, but rather bitter. Bitter words do not bring peace or make things better.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: “Rasuãdze kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔɖo ɔ̃ ratedze, fiɛ ɔɖabo kuwɛ̃ ana iisɛ ɔɖo ɔ̃ ɔkɔse.
translated: The squirrel eats palm nuts in the farm, but it eats corn at home.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: Kapɛrɛ kere masɛ marɔdɛ̃ kunagini kunyɔ, ɣɛɛ maisɛ maɖoe kukawɛ̃ si mi Ɔse gɔ i Kato iitɔrã.
translated: When the rain stops falling, the women go to the farm to harvest yams for the festival in Kato village.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: Ne ɔso midaanigã alasɔ mifɔ kuɣa miɖo kunagini. Idaakpɛ̃-ɔ Kunuarɛ Sɔ Aɖe Yesu Ɔre.
translated: He/she always wakes up early and goes to the farm. Today is Sunday, the day of Jesus’ resurrection.

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: “Midaabu sɔ isobuɛ iɖe losu lobɔ kayiiso. Ooɣo! Iɣɛɣɛso iɖe lobɔ.
translated: "The children are playing under the big tree. Look! They are climbing the tree."

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: Ɔbiara gɔ loabie sɔ ɔ̃akɔkɔ ɔ̃ ngbã to ɔ̃ayura me, ɣɛɛ ɔbiara gɔ loasu ɔ̃ ngbã ɔtã ala wũ ɔso to ɔ̃ana ngbã. Ɣaa To Ɔ̃atã Ɔbiara Gɔ Lofɔ Yesu Masiaidze Kuso.
translated: Everyone who believes in him will not perish but will have eternal life, and everyone who does not believe will be condemned. This is because only those who believe in Jesus the Messiah are saved.

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: Gɔ Yohanes ɔba ɔ̃ito ɔɖe ara ɔnɛ ndã mɛ loamɔɛ̃ ne, maturi sɔ siwarã lalaa mpia i ɔ̃ ame.
translated: When John’s son was coming from the farm that day, he saw three people sitting under a big tree.

ref: You people are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other,
text: “Kiniɔ ne, be loasu nɔme maturi lokateragu? Mase lɛ mabiɛtɛ ma nto maɣa kaɣa i kuɣa ame. Ma malaa to maɣere ma sɔ,
translated: So then, why do you always complain? I told you already that I will not leave you alone. I will stay with you always, you hear,

ref: When Jesus found out what was happening, he left there and large crowds followed him. He healed all of their sick,
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔnɔ sɔ maɔbara adzuni sɔ maaɖoe wũ ne, ɔrui i mmɔ. Maturi gbodzoo ɔsiai wũ fiɛ ɔsa manyɛse ɔɖuɖu.
translated: When Jesus saw the large crowd, he had compassion on them and healed their sick. As evening approached, the disciples came to him.

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Mi ma lonyanyarĩ lɛ maɣɛ! Nda miabara fiɛ miawo ara sɛɛ ɔɣɛ gɔ miɖe maturi lalaa? Alasɔ nnɛ loyi ɔturi ɔtu ame i kanya sɛ kaɣɛ.
translated: We are very happy today! Did you all sleep well and are you ready for the new day? Let us now go and greet the people in the house.

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Mmara matedze mawɛ̃ gu ma Farisise mawɛ̃ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Ratedze, boto bobie sɔ bara iwawãra irere si bofɔ-ɔ boɖe.”
translated: One day some Pharisees came and asked him, “Teacher, is it right for a man to divorce his wife for any reason?”

ref: Then he taught them many things by using stories. He said: A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: Ɔte ma ara gbodzoo i agbã ame. Ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Kpɛ̃dze ɔwɛ̃ ɔsɛ ɔraɖui kasakɔ̃.
translated: He himself saw the person first. He said, "Look, here comes a stranger."

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more. But people who don't have anything will lose even what little they have.
text: Alasɔ ngɔ mba ara ne, maatã wũ ara ɔ̃ ara ɔsi abua, ɣɛɛ ngɔ ina ne, kɛkɛĩ gɔ ame ɔba ne, maamɔɛ̃ mafɔ wũ iso.
translated: If you wake up in the morning, greet your father and your mother, then go outside and greet the people you meet, and your day will be good.

ref: But the girl's mother told her to say, “Here on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Ɔbirɔ̃gomi ɔnyi ɔɣere wũ sɔ ɔkarɛ Ndupiedze Yohanes iti. Ne ɔso ɔsɛ ɔ̃aɣere igara sɔ, “Tã me Ndupiedze Yohanes iti i kategelei iso ngbe kiniɔ.”
translated: The elders sent a woman to call John the Baptist. When she arrived where he was, she said, “They have sent me to call you to come to the elders.”

ref: But they said, “We have only five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
text: Ne marasuãdze ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Abolo abo aru gu akpɛ fɛ̃a anyɔ kere nɖe aɖera wa boba.”
translated: The woman said to her child, “Abolo, go and bring the basket that is hanging on the wall.”

ref: and he told the crowd to sit down on the grass. Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples, and they gave it to the people.
text: Ɔɣere maturi sɔ masɛ i karɔ̃ i sikua iso fiɛ ka ɔsu abolo abo aru gu akpɛ fɛ̃a anyɔ, ɔnyɔ kato ɔpɛ Ɣaa siba. Ɔbɛbɛse ɔsu ɔtã ɔ̃ marasuãdze sɔ masu maɣɛɣɛ maturi.
translated: The woman wakes up early in the morning and goes to the farm to harvest yams before the sun rises, so that she can sell them at the Gaa market. She says that if the sun becomes hot, it is difficult to work on the farm.

ref: The men in the boat worshiped Jesus and said, “You really are the Son of God!” Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Mark
text: Ne ɔso marasuãdze ma i ɔkolo ame ɔpɛ agɛgɛ̃ matã Yesu maɣɛ sɔ, “Gbaã ne, Ɣaa Ɔbi aɖe!” Yesu Ɔsa Manyɛse I Genezaret.
translated: When he finished speaking, he said to Simon, “Put out into deep water, and let down the nets for a catch.” Jesus Calls His First Disciples at the Lake of Gennesaret.

ref: Jesus answered, “Every plant that my Father in heaven did not plant will be pulled up by the roots.
text: Yesu ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Kudziri biara gɔ iiɖe Tete gɔ i kato lopia ne, maarui kɔ̃ mapɛyu.
translated: Jesus often said to the people, “Every seed that is planted in the ground must die first before it can grow and bear fruit.

ref: Is this any way to show respect to your parents? You ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching.
text: Ne ɔso iki i mi kurabarara iso ne, miɔsɛ̃ Ɣaa mmara, mimɔɛ̃ mi mawa mire mito.
translated: If you see my friend over there, tell him that I have gone, but I will return soon.

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
text: Ɣɛɛ Yesu iiɣɛ kuira. Ne ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔba maaɣere wũ sɔ, “Sa wũ si ɔnyua kulu ɔla ɔtɛ̃ra bo atsue!”
translated: Jesus answered him. And he said to his disciples, "If anyone wants to follow me, he must deny himself!"

ref: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.
text: Ɣɛɛ atɔ̃me wa nsɛ abɔrɛ i ɔturi kanya ame ne, ɔ̃ ɔtu ame abɔrɛ fiɛ asɛ apukutura wũ.
translated: If someone does not help another person in their time of trouble, they should not expect help when they are in trouble themselves.

ref: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
text: Yesu ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Iilɛ sɔ maasu mabiɛtɛ aɖera matã makpɛkpɛ.”
translated: Jesus said to them, “You must give them something to eat.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔnɔ atɔ̃me wagbe ne, ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Ɔrɔ̃go, fɔ kafɔkaɖe ɔmɔ gbaã! Nnɛ ato akarɛ si iba itã-ɔ.” Ɔluwɛ̃ ɔbi birɔ̃gomi ko losarɛ. Yesu Ɔsa Maturi Gbodzoo.
translated: When Jesus was at the table, he took bread, gave thanks, and said, “Take, eat; this is my body which is given for you! Do this in remembrance of me.” They all ate together. Jesus the Savior is risen.

ref: When they opened their eyes, they saw only Jesus.
text: Gɔ matara anɔ kato ne, mainya kuwɛ̃ i mmɔ, iɖo Yesu ɔnɔwɛ̃.
translated: If you believe in your heart, you will be saved, because of Jesus.

ref: In fact, he has already come. But the people did not recognize him and treated him just as they wanted to. They will soon make the Son of Man suffer in the same way.”
text: Ɣɛɛ ne, nto loɣere mi sɔ Eliya ɔɔba koko, maturi iinya wũ maɖi ite. Mabara wũ nnɛ mabie fiɛ ngbɔ ame mato maabara Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi ɔtile ana ne.”
translated: So then, when the rooster crows, Eliya gets up early, prepares his things and goes out. He says that today he will go to the place where the Chief of Awune lives.

ref: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
text: “Nto loledza mi iɣere sɔ ira biara nɛ i mi maturi inyɔ aatɔrã misia ita i kayi gagbe iso i kayi ikparama ame ne, wũ Ɔse gɔ i kato to ɔ̃abara ne ɔtã mi.
translated: If you do not forgive your fellow human being from your heart, then God will also not forgive you your sins.

ref: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
text: Gɔ ɔɖabo gɔmɔ na sikã ɔ̃atã kuso ɔso ne, igara ɔtã sɔ marɔdɛ̃ ɔ̃ gu ɔ̃ ɔre gu mabi lɛ masande gu ira biara nɛ ɔba si masu matã kuso.
translated: If you want to get money, you must work hard, because it is not possible to get something good without effort, and you should not expect to get anything if you do not work for it.

ref: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
text: “Ne ɔso igara ɔpia makpere ɔɖabo mabɔ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, ‘Ɔɖabo lalaa aɖe! Losu fɔ kuso gɔ ato me ɔɖuɖu lotsɛ-ɔ alasɔ atã me iti.
translated: He took his basket and said to his wife, ‘Wife, I am going! Prepare food and put it in the basket so I can eat it on the way.’

ref: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
text: Ma ara ɔɖo Yesu kayiri, ne ɔpɛgu ma anɔmi matsɛ inya fiɛ marui masiai wũ ne.
translated: If I follow Jesus truly, then I will not walk in darkness but will have the light of life.

ref: But Jesus said to them, “If you have faith and don't doubt, I promise you can do what I did to this tree. And you will be able to do even more. You can tell this mountain to get up and jump into the sea, and it will.
text: Ne Yesu ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Nto loɣere mi gbaã sɔ si mifɔ miɖe fiɛ miiwararã ne, mito miawo nnɛ lobara kudziri gɔgbe ɔbara. Fiɛ iiɖe ne kere, ɣɛɛ mito miaɣere kube gɔgbe sɔ, ‘Kɛlɛ aafuĩdza so apia i ɔpo ame,’ fiɛ iaba ngbɔ.
translated: But Jesus said to them, “No one who puts his hand to the plow and looks back is fit for service in the kingdom of God.”

ref: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
text: Alasɔ Ndupiedze Yohanes ɔba ɔ̃ate mi sibɔlɔlɔ ɔri, ɣɛɛ miifɔ wũ miɖe, ɣɛɛ ma lampofɔdze gu asɔrɔ̃maɖedze ɔfɔ wũ maɖe. Atoa sɔ minya nwagbe ne, miifinikira mi adzuni mifɔ wũ miɖe.” Yesu Ɔpɛ Igbã I Marabaradze Lalaa Mawɛ̃ Iso.
translated: After John’s messengers left, Jesus began to speak to the crowd about John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed swayed by the wind? If not, what did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? No, those who wear expensive clothes and indulge in luxury are in palaces. But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.

ref: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
text: Matã mmuai sɔ, “Ɔto ɔ̃aɖoe marabaradze lalaa magbe ɔsu kakpɛkɔ̃ ɔtã marabaradze mama ma loasu ɔ̃ kaɖekɔ̃ mabɔ wũ i ɔwi iso si masɔrɛ̃ ɔraɖui.”

text: Ne ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Minyɔ ara wagbe ɔɖuɖu kukaakɔ. Alasɔ gbaã nto loɣere mi sɔ mato maabiɛbiɛ wã ɔɖuɖu gɔ ita kuiwɛ̃ iibakuti isia i ne ɔlaa iso sɔ maibasisi mapɛyu.” Yesu Ɔɣere Ɔ̃ Marasuãdze Ɔɣiã Gɔ Maaɖe.

translated: He took bread and gave thanks, broke it and gave it to them, saying, this is my body, which is given for you; do this in remembrance of me. In the same way, after the meal, he took the cup and said, this cup is the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for you.

ref: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!
text: So si siɣɔ mma lobɔ so karɔ̃, alasɔ ma loaɖe kayiiso ara ne.
translated: If you want to eat the fruit, you must first climb the tree.

ref: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
text: So si siɣɔ mma situ ame lokpa, ala ma loanya Ɣaa ne.
translated: If you see someone in trouble, you should help them.

ref: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
text: “Mi nse lɛ mma mitã maturi ɔɖuɖu, ɣɛɛ si mma ɔyu me ɔmɛrɛ̃ ne, maisibawo ɔri kuwɛ̃ iso ɔki ɔpia me ɔmɛrɛ̃. Misiina kusia kukuwɛ̃ iɖo sɔ maasu me mawiri maturi ɔsɛ me iso.
translated: We went to the farm early in the morning, and while we were working there, some people came and called us to come home quickly. After that, we washed our hands and returned to continue the work on the farm.

ref: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
text: “Loiba lotindza Mose mmara gu Ɣaa kanyamaɖidze ara ite ɖuɖuuɖu, ɣɛɛ loba sɔ si wã ibiara aba i ne ame.
translated: When Moses finished speaking all these words to all Israel, he said to them:

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: “Ɔwiɔ ne minɔ masɛ maɣɛ ana sɔ, ‘Daatu ndamu, Ɣɛɛ bara i nnɛ aɣɛ sɔ aabara atã Ɣaa iso.’
translated: The chief said to me, ‘Don’t worry, come here tomorrow and I will give you three yams.’

ref: You know you have been taught, “An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.”
text: “Minɔ masɛ maɣɛ sɔ, ‘Si abiɛ fɔ ɔlaa inɔmi ne, maabiɛ fɔ ire, fiɛ si akpa fɔ ɔlaa inyɛ ne, maakpa fɔ ire fɔ wũ.’
translated: My father told me, ‘If you do good to someone, do it with your right hand, but if you do bad, do it with your left hand so you can hide it.’

ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Si misɛ miɖɔɛ mma nsɛ maɖɔɛ mi ɔnɔwɛ̃ ne, be kuso miafɔ i Ɣaa kɔrɛ? Ma lampofɔdze ma wũ masɛ mabara ngbɔ.
translated: If I go there and I don't find you, where should I wait for you? Please show me so that I don't get lost.

ref: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
text: Ne ɔso si abuai wɛrɛba ne, daatã nane fɔ ɔlaa gɔ lomatã-ɔ si ɔtsue nnɛ abara,
translated: If someone goes to the farm early in the morning, they will return home before the sun gets hot.

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: “Fiɛ si mito mikparama kayi ne, midaase lɛ mma nsɛ mabara ara si mabo maturi anɔ. Masɛ mabie sɔ maata maya makparama kayi i kasɔrɛkɔ̃ gu kuɣa ame sɔ maturi si manya ma. Lobie sɔ lotã mi itsue sɔ ma kuso ame maɔfɔ mmɔ ne.
translated: When I take my basket out, I am grateful that I do not return empty-handed. Even if I only find a little, I put it in my basket and thank the one who gave it to me. It is better to have something small than to have nothing at all.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: Ɣɛɛ fɔ si ato akparama kayi ne, kɛlɛ fɔ iyo kame si asɛ̃ iyo apia so fiɛ aakpere fɔ Ɔse gɔ maisɛ manya ala anɔmi, iyɔ gɔ ɔ̃ ɔsɛ ɔnya ta awɛrɛra ne, ɔto ɔ̃atã-ɔ kuso.
translated: If someone is given a new pot, but the person who gave it to them is not around to see it being used, it is said that the pot is not truly theirs, it is just being borrowed.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: Fɔ sigara iɖe si siba i bo ndɛ̃. Maabara fɔ kuɖɔɛ i kayiiso lɛ kumɛgɔ masɛ mabara i kato.
translated: If you smoke cigarettes, it can harm your body. It is better to stop so that you can live a healthy life.

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: Ne loatã sɔ maturi iibatsue sɔ ato anyi kanya, ɣɛɛ fɔ Ɔse gɔ maisɛ manya, fiɛ ɔsɛ ɔnya ara wɛrɛa aatã-ɔ kuso. Kato Nɖe Ara Ka Kɔlakɔ̃ Sɛɛ.
translated: If you want to live a good life in this world, you must listen to God and do what is right, so that you will have peace everywhere you go. This is what our elders have taught us.

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: Ɣɛɛ nnɛ iso i mi anɔ si arɛtɛ nɖe sɔ Ɣaa si ɔɖe sigara i mi iso gu si mibara sibɔlɔlɔ ara iyɔ ɔto ɔ̃asu ara wagbe ɔɖuɖu ɔtã mi.
translated: But today I want to tell you about something that Gaa, who used to smoke cigarettes, told me when we were sitting together under the big tree at the edge of the village.

ref: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
text: Ne ɔso nto loɣere mi sɔ midaamararã so i mi ngbã, nnɛ miaɖe gu nnɛ mianɛ, ɣee mi sosina gu nnɛ miapia iso. Ɛɛbɛrɛ ngbã iifɔ kuɣa miɖo aɖera fiɛ kuturidziri iifɔ kuɣa kuɖo arapia?
translated: If you wake up in the morning and you remember what you dreamt, today you tell it, today you forget it, or you keep it and later you use it. But why do some people always remember their dreams and others always forget them?

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: “Be ɔso mito mimararã so i nnɛ miapia iso? Minyɔ ipo ame adziri kumɛgɔ asɛ amɔ. Aisɛ ata ɖɔti ɣee awe sikati.
translated: When I went to the farm today, what did I see? I saw some people harvesting yams in the field. They said the harvest this year will be very good.

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: Ara wagbe i maɣɔsɔrɛdze sɛ mabie ne, ɣɛɛ mi Ɔse gɔ i kato ɣe sɔ nwagbe ɔɖuɖu ɔnya mi.
translated: When I was about to leave my mother said, go and greet your father before you leave.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Ɣee ɔsu aɣɛ ɔtã ɔ̃ ɔbi si ɔkarɛ wũ ikpɛ?
translated: If it rains, will you still go to the farm?

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Mmɔ loaɣere ma sɔ, ‘Loiɣe mi kukakɔi! Mi ara lalaa mabaradze, mirui me i kɔrɛ.’ Yesu Ɔte Ara I Ayo Matsuedze Agbã Anyɔ Iso.
translated: Then the woman said, ‘Lord help me! I have nothing left, my children and I are hungry.’ Jesus is the Bread of Life who gives us food.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
text: Kado ɔpɛ, siwore ɔyi, awɛ ɔkpɛ̃ teteree ɣɛɛ iyo iirɔ̃ ala mapia ne karɔ̃ ame kukaakɔ.
translated: When it rains, the river rises, so people who live nearby move to higher ground.

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Ne kutsuɛ ne, makɔ maturi gbodzoo ma i siwarã lalaa si i iso mabɔ Yesu. Ɔsu itɔ̃me iwɛ̃ ɔsa siwarã lalaa ɔɖi i ma ame fiɛ ɔsa mma ɔɖuɖu nto manyɛ.
translated: That night, I had a dream in which you were among people who believe in Jesus. In the morning, I saw you among those same people and I knew you were one of them.

ref: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔsɛ Petro iyo ne, ɔnya sɔ Petro masɔrɔ̃go rɛ, ɔbe ɔtɔ ɔto ɔnyɛ.
translated: When Jesus called Peter, Peter was afraid, but he did not refuse.

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
text: Mmaratedze ɔwɛ̃ ɔba ɔ̃atu wũ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Ratedze, loasiai-ɔ kakɔi biara ga aakɛlɛ.”
translated: The elder said to the child who was crying, “Child, don’t worry, everything will be alright.”

ref: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
text: Marasuãdze ɔbore mabo wũ matara wũ sɔ, “Bosate, ɖi bo ne ɔkpi boto!”
translated: Marasuãdze took his machete and said, “Friend, let’s go and cut palm nuts!”

ref: a terrible storm suddenly struck the lake, and waves started splashing into their boat. Jesus was sound asleep,
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ ne, awɛ siare awɛ̃ ɔtsɛ ikpɛ̃ i itubu iso gɔ itã ndu to mita mibo i ɔkolo ame, ɣɛɛ Yesu rɛ ɔto ɔrɛ sirɛ.
translated: Long ago, when people were still living in darkness and did not know the way to God, God sent his son, Jesus, to show them the way.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Makparako ikuri iwɛ̃ ɣɛ i ndɛ̃ mɛmɔ isɛ mato maɖe ara.
translated: Makparako said that he would go to the farm if it did not rain.

ref: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
text: Siwarã lalaa ɔtã Yesu iti sɔ, “Si aasa bo ne, tã bosɛ boabo i makparako ikuri nɛ nɣɛ i mmɔ ame.”
translated: Jesus said to them, "If you love me, then you will do what I have told you to do."

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
text: Yesu ɔtsue nnɛ mpia i ma adzuni ame ne ɔso ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Be ɔso mito misusu adzuni nyanyarĩa wagbe i mi situ ame?
translated: Jesus sat down opposite the offering box and watched the people putting money into it. He said, "How much are these people putting into the offering box?"

ref: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔbo iyo ame ne, maba ɔ̃ kɔrɛ, ne ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Mifɔ miɖe sɔ loawo mi ɔsa?” Matã mmuai sɔ, “Aĩ, Bosate!”
translated: When Jesus saw the man, he went closer and asked him, “Do you want me to heal you?” The sick man replied, “Yes, Lord!”

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
text: Ne Ɔso ɔɣere ɔ̃ marasuãdze sɔ, “Ɔraɖui gɔ maasɔrɛ̃ ɔsi, ɣɛɛ marabaradze iisi.
translated: The woman said to her husband, "Come and eat with us, and let us be happy together."

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: “Ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ne, maturi to maaɖi ma manyii matã sɔ maɖoe fiɛ mase ana to maaɖi ma mabi matã. Mabi to maata maya ma maɣese iso maɖi ma matã maɖoe.
translated: When someone dies, we gather together and mourn for the person who has left us. We stay together and comfort each other in our sorrow.

ref: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
text: Wũ ɔso ne, mato maamɔɛ̃ mi makɛlɛgu matɔ̃ɖedze gu magara katɔ̃ sɔ maɣɛgu mi atɔ̃me, ɣɛɛ miɣɛ Itɔ̃me Bielea i mma loiɖe ma Yudase katɔ̃.
translated: After he had gone, I went to the mountain and prayed for a long time, then I returned to the house where the disciples were staying in Judea.

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: Alasɔ iiɖe mi loaɖe ika, ɣɛɛ mi Ɔse Siwarã loaɖe ika sikigu i mi iso.
translated: If you want to learn this language, you should go to Siwarã to learn it well.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: “Rasuãdze kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔɖo ɔ̃ ratedze, fiɛ ɔɖabo kuwɛ̃ ana iisɛ ɔɖo ɔ̃ ɔkɔse.
translated: Rats eat corn when it is ripe, but they do not eat it when it is still unripe.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: Kapɛrɛ kere masɛ marɔdɛ̃ kunagini kunyɔ, ɣɛɛ maisɛ maɖoe kukawɛ̃ si mi Ɔse gɔ i Kato iitɔrã.
translated: When the cock crows in the morning, the elders say that Ɔse is going to Kato to buy salt.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: Ɣɛɛ ta mi siwɛrɛ̃ dzɛ i mi ati iso ne, Ɣaa ɣe sɛ̃ kukame.
translated: If you want to learn this language well, you must be very patient.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: Ne ɔso midaanigã alasɔ mifɔ kuɣa miɖo kunagini. Idaakpɛ̃-ɔ Kunuarɛ Sɔ Aɖe Yesu Ɔre.
translated: On the third day after I woke up, I went to the farm. That day was Sunday, the day of Jesus.

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: “Midaabu sɔ isobuɛ iɖe losu lobɔ kayiiso. Ooɣo! Iɣɛɣɛso iɖe lobɔ.
translated: "The woman is pounding fufu in the room. Oh! She is in the room."

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: Ɔbiara gɔ loabie sɔ ɔ̃akɔkɔ ɔ̃ ngbã to ɔ̃ayura me, ɣɛɛ ɔbiara gɔ loasu ɔ̃ ngbã ɔtã ala wũ ɔso to ɔ̃ana ngbã. Ɣaa To Ɔ̃atã Ɔbiara Gɔ Lofɔ Yesu Masiaidze Kuso.
translated: Anyone who believes in him will not perish but have eternal life, just as anyone who does not believe will not have life. This is because only those who believe in Jesus the Messiah are saved.

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: Ɔbiara gɔ loafɔ Ɣaa kanyaɖidze ne, ɔto ɔ̃ana kaɖekɔ̃ i kanyaɖidze kuso ame. Ngɔ lofɔ ɔturi bɔlɔlɔa ala ɔ̃ sibɔlɔlɔ ɔso ne, ɔto ɔ̃afɔ sibɔlɔlɔ kuso.
translated: Everyone loves to eat sweet things, but not everyone who eats sweet things is a good person. If you love playing football but you do not play football well, you are not a good football player.

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: Gɔ Yohanes ɔba ɔ̃ito ɔɖe ara ɔnɛ ndã mɛ loamɔɛ̃ ne, maturi sɔ siwarã lalaa mpia i ɔ̃ ame.
translated: When John’s son was still very young, he could not do heavy work like an adult.

ref: You people are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other,
text: “Kiniɔ ne, be loasu nɔme maturi lokateragu? Mase lɛ mabiɛtɛ ma nto maɣa kaɣa i kuɣa ame. Ma malaa to maɣere ma sɔ,
translated: So then, why did you go to the market yesterday? I told you not to go there with those people. I am not happy with what you did.

ref: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
text: Ɣɛɛ gɔ mme, Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi, ɔba fiɛ loto loɖe ara lonɛ ndã ne, maturi to maɣɛ sɔ, ‘Minyɔ ɖeɖei gu tarɛ̃dze gɔgbe! Ɔɖe ma lampofɔdze gu makpibaradze ɔlaa.’ Ɣɛɛ ne, Ɣaa iwola sɛ ite so i ɔturi nsɛgbai ame.” Simagɛ̃ Siwɛ̃ Maturi Iifɔ Yesu Maɖe.
translated: One day, Teacher Awune Obi, coming back from the farm, saw a boy sitting by the roadside crying, and asked him, ‘Why are you crying so bitterly, my child?’ The boy replied, ‘I have lost my lamp and my schoolbag.’ That is how Teacher Awune Obi became known for his kindness.”

ref: Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
text: Alasɔ gbaã ne Tete, ngbɔ ikote-ɔ ne.
translated: Alasɔ went to Tete, he did not return.

ref: Then Jesus told the man, “Hold out your hand.” The man did, and it became as healthy as the other one.
text: Ne Yesu ɔɣere ngɔ kɔrɔ̃ lokarĩ sɔ, “Nabarã fɔ kɔrɔ̃.” Ɔnabarã kɔ̃, ɔluwɛ̃ ne, ilɛ wũ kɔrɔ̃ karĩa ɔse lɛ ngɔ nsarɛ.
translated: When Jesus had gone out to the shore, he said, “Let’s go to the other side.” They went, and as they were going, he fell asleep in the boat while they were crossing.

ref: When Jesus found out what was happening, he left there and large crowds followed him. He healed all of their sick,
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔnɔ sɔ maɔbara adzuni sɔ maaɖoe wũ ne, ɔrui i mmɔ. Maturi gbodzoo ɔsiai wũ fiɛ ɔsa manyɛse ɔɖuɖu.
translated: When Jesus saw the crowd coming towards him, he went up a hill. After sitting down, his disciples came to him.

ref: but warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: Ɣɛɛ ɔsɛ̃ ɔtã ma sɔ madaaɖi wũ mate ngɔ ɔɖe.
translated: If you want to go, go but don't say I didn't tell you.

ref: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
text: “Wũ ɔɖabo gɔ loɖi lotã so i ngbe ne. Ɔmɔɛ̃ me ɔtu fiɛ loipia wũ kuɣɛ. Loafedza wũ Siwarã lofere wũ iso sɔ ɔ̃apɛ wũ atɔ̃me iɣɛgu imomo ɔtã abuiti ɔɖuɖu.
translated: If you wake up early in the morning, you will hear the birds singing. The sun rises gently and the sky becomes bright. Children from Siwarã village run to the river to bathe and play before going to the farm with their parents.

ref: If you are not on my side, you are against me. If you don't gather in the harvest with me, you scatter it.
text: “Ngɔ loiɣɛ me i kama ne, ɔta ɔɣɛ me iso, fiɛ ngɔ loito ɔsagu me ara kanya ne, ara ɔto ɔsamarã.
translated: If you love me, stay with me, and if you don't love me, let me go so I can find someone who does.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Yesu ɣe ma adzuni, ne ɔso ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Karɔ̃ biara ga maturi loɣɛɣɛ so fiɛ mata maɣɛ so iso aakpese ikpakpi. Ngbɔ ame i ɔmagɛ̃ ɣee iyo biara nɛ maturi loɣɛɣɛ so fiɛ mata maɣɛ so iso iibawo ɔya ne.
translated: Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, unless a grain of wheat falls to the ground and dies, it remains only a single seed. But if it dies, it produces many seeds.”

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Mi ma lonyanyarĩ lɛ maɣɛ! Nda miabara fiɛ miawo ara sɛɛ ɔɣɛ gɔ miɖe maturi lalaa? Alasɔ nnɛ loyi ɔturi ɔtu ame i kanya sɛ kaɣɛ.
translated: We are very grateful today! Did you all sleep well and wake up strong? Today is a good day for everyone to do their work well.

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Mmara matedze mawɛ̃ gu ma Farisise mawɛ̃ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Ratedze, boto bobie sɔ bara iwawãra irere si bofɔ-ɔ boɖe.”
translated: One day, some Pharisees said to him, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign.”

ref: Then he taught them many things by using stories. He said: A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: Ɔte ma ara gbodzoo i agbã ame. Ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Kpɛ̃dze ɔwɛ̃ ɔsɛ ɔraɖui kasakɔ̃.
translated: He himself saw the stranger. He asked, “Why are you walking alone at night?”

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more. But people who don't have anything will lose even what little they have.
text: Alasɔ ngɔ mba ara ne, maatã wũ ara ɔ̃ ara ɔsi abua, ɣɛɛ ngɔ ina ne, kɛkɛĩ gɔ ame ɔba ne, maamɔɛ̃ mafɔ wũ iso.
translated: If you have a friend, treat them well, because in this world, it is people who help people, and that is what makes life good.

ref: The Son of Man will send out his angels, and they will gather from his kingdom everyone who does wrong or causes others to sin.
text: Mme, Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi, to loapia wũ makpabo sɔ masa mma ɔɖuɖu lokɔ maturi mapia i ikpi ame gu mma lobara ara lalaa kanya.
translated: Yes, Mr. Awune Obi, when you go to the farm, you must be careful so that you do not step on a snake or injure yourself with a hoe or cutlass.

ref: Then he will throw them into a flaming furnace, where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
text: Mato maasu ma mafuĩdza mapia i itɔkpe nɛ nto itsue ame ngbegɔ maabiɛ kaku maɖo arɔĩ makpa.
translated: New teachers and students must always respect the rules of the school.

ref: Jesus asked his disciples if they understood all these things. They said, “Yes, we do.”
text: Yesu ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Minɔ ara wa loɣere mi i ngbe karɔ̃?” Matã mmuai sɔ, “Aĩ, bonɔ.”
translated: Jesus said to her, “Do you believe this?” She answered, “Yes, Lord.”

ref: But the girl's mother told her to say, “Here on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Ɔbirɔ̃gomi ɔnyi ɔɣere wũ sɔ ɔkarɛ Ndupiedze Yohanes iti. Ne ɔso ɔsɛ ɔ̃aɣere igara sɔ, “Tã me Ndupiedze Yohanes iti i kategelei iso ngbe kiniɔ.”
translated: The messenger went to the place where John the Baptist was. And he said to him, “John the Baptist has sent me to ask you this question.”

ref: But they said, “We have only five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
text: Ne marasuãdze ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Abolo abo aru gu akpɛ fɛ̃a anyɔ kere nɖe aɖera wa boba.”
translated: The woman said to her child, “Abolo, go and bring me the basket that is hanging behind the door.”

ref: and he told the crowd to sit down on the grass. Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples, and they gave it to the people.
text: Ɔɣere maturi sɔ masɛ i karɔ̃ i sikua iso fiɛ ka ɔsu abolo abo aru gu akpɛ fɛ̃a anyɔ, ɔnyɔ kato ɔpɛ Ɣaa siba. Ɔbɛbɛse ɔsu ɔtã ɔ̃ marasuãdze sɔ masu maɣɛɣɛ maturi.
translated: The woman said that she will go to the farm tomorrow morning and if it rains, she will not go. She also said that if she does not go, she will send someone else to the farm.

ref: The men in the boat worshiped Jesus and said, “You really are the Son of God!” Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Mark
text: Ne ɔso marasuãdze ma i ɔkolo ame ɔpɛ agɛgɛ̃ matã Yesu maɣɛ sɔ, “Gbaã ne, Ɣaa Ɔbi aɖe!” Yesu Ɔsa Manyɛse I Genezaret.
translated: When he finished speaking, he said to Simon, “Put out into deep water, and let down the nets for a catch.” Jesus Calls His First Disciples at Gennesaret.

ref: Jesus answered, “Every plant that my Father in heaven did not plant will be pulled up by the roots.
text: Yesu ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Kudziri biara gɔ iiɖe Tete gɔ i kato lopia ne, maarui kɔ̃ mapɛyu.
translated: Jesus often told the people, "No one puts new wine into old wineskins, otherwise they will burst."

ref: Is this any way to show respect to your parents? You ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching.
text: Ne ɔso iki i mi kurabarara iso ne, miɔsɛ̃ Ɣaa mmara, mimɔɛ̃ mi mawa mire mito.
translated: He/she/they did not come to the market today, because Gaa said, I should stay at home and not go.

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
text: Ɣɛɛ Yesu iiɣɛ kuira. Ne ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔba maaɣere wũ sɔ, “Sa wũ si ɔnyua kulu ɔla ɔtɛ̃ra bo atsue!”
translated: And Jesus answered him. But he said to his disciples, "If you have faith as small as a mustard seed, you can say to this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' and it will move!"

ref: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.
text: Ɣɛɛ atɔ̃me wa nsɛ abɔrɛ i ɔturi kanya ame ne, ɔ̃ ɔtu ame abɔrɛ fiɛ asɛ apukutura wũ.
translated: If someone does not help another person in their time of trouble, they should not expect help when they are in trouble.

ref: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
text: Yesu ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Iilɛ sɔ maasu mabiɛtɛ aɖera matã makpɛkpɛ.”
translated: Jesus said to them, “You must give them something to eat.”

ref: “Lord, this is true,” the woman said, “but even puppies get the crumbs that fall from their owner's table.”
text: Ɔrɔ̃go ɔtã wũ mmuai sɔ, “Wũ Sate gbaã iɖe, ɣɛɛ makpɛkpɛ sɛ marɛrɛ aɖera wa lokpakpase i makɔse ɔpɔrɔ̃ iso afere i karɔ̃ maɖe.”
translated: The elder woman said to the children, "You Sate's children, listen well so I will tell you a story that happened a long time ago."

ref: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔnɔ atɔ̃me wagbe ne, ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Ɔrɔ̃go, fɔ kafɔkaɖe ɔmɔ gbaã! Nnɛ ato akarɛ si iba itã-ɔ.” Ɔluwɛ̃ ɔbi birɔ̃gomi ko losarɛ. Yesu Ɔsa Maturi Gbodzoo.
translated: When Jesus was at the table, he took bread, gave thanks, and said, “Take, eat; this is my body given for you! Do this in remembrance of me.” They all ate together. Jesus is the Bread of Life.

ref: From there, Jesus went along Lake Galilee. Then he climbed a hill and sat down.
text: Yesu ɔrui i mmɔ ɔsɛ Galilea itubu kuruɛkuruɛ ɔsɛ ɔ̃aya kabemi kawɛ̃ ɔkpase ɔsɛ i karɔ̃.
translated: Jesus began to go around Galilee, preaching the good news and saying that people should repent.

ref: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
text: Yesu ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Abolo kuboi kurɛ̃ mito?” Ne matã wũ mmuai sɔ, “Abolo kuboi kukɔdzɛ gu kufarai wiriwiria kurɛ̃saã.”
translated: Jesus asked them, “How many loaves do you have?” They replied, “We have seven loaves and a few small fish.”

ref: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
text: Ita i ɔwi gɔmɔ ne, Yesu ɔtsɛ ɔ̃ marasuãdze iɣere iɖi sɔ, “Nto loakɛlɛ Yerusalem fiɛ loawe inyɛ gbaã i makpakpa, masɔrɛdze makpakpa gu mmara matedze nrɔɔ̃ ame. Mato maaɖoe me, ɣɛɛ ikpɛnɛ tɛare ne, Ɣaa to ɔ̃atara me lowe ngbã.”
translated: When he came near, Jesus saw the city and wept over it, saying, “If you, even you, had only known on this day what would bring you peace—but now it is hidden from your eyes. The days will come upon you when your enemies will build an embankment against you and encircle you and hem you in on every side. They will dash you to the ground, you and the children within your walls. They will not leave one stone on another, because you did not recognize the time of God’s coming to you.”

ref: The Son of Man will soon come in the glory of his Father and with his angels to reward all people for what they have done.
text: Alasɔ Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi to ɔ̃aba i ɔ̃ Ɔse ikpawaĩ ame gu ɔ̃ makpabo fiɛ ɔ̃atã ɔbiara kuso i ɔ̃ karabara kanya.
translated: Once upon a time, a woman gave birth to a child but she did not have anyone to help her so she raised the child alone.

ref: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?”
text: Mmɔ i Yesu to ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Ne mi ɣɛɛ? Nna mibu sɔ loɖe?”
translated: Then Jesus turned and asked, “What do you want? What are you looking for?”

ref: When they opened their eyes, they saw only Jesus.
text: Gɔ matara anɔ kato ne, mainya kuwɛ̃ i mmɔ, iɖo Yesu ɔnɔwɛ̃.
translated: If you have any problem, pray, and Jesus will help you.

ref: In fact, he has already come. But the people did not recognize him and treated him just as they wanted to. They will soon make the Son of Man suffer in the same way.”
text: Ɣɛɛ ne, nto loɣere mi sɔ Eliya ɔɔba koko, maturi iinya wũ maɖi ite. Mabara wũ nnɛ mabie fiɛ ngbɔ ame mato maabara Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi ɔtile ana ne.”
translated: So then, if you hear that Elijah is coming, prepare yourselves and do not be afraid. Because today we are going to the house of the Lord to pray together.

ref: Later the disciples went to Jesus in private and asked him, “Why couldn't we force out the demon?”
text: Ne marasuãdze ɔbore mabo wũ makarɛ wũ pɔkɔsɔɔ sɔ, “Be ɔso fiɛ boiwo siwarã lalaa ɔsa ɔɖi?”
translated: He asked the old woman and her children, “Who among you will go to the farm tomorrow?”

ref: If your eye causes you to sin, poke it out and get rid of it. You would be better off to go into life with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fires of hell. The Lost Sheep (Luke
text: Si fɔ inɔmi ana nsɛ itã abo i ikpi ame ne, were ne ɖi pɛyu! Ilɛ iɖo sɔ aawe inɔmi iwɛ̃ abo i ngbã ame iɖo sɔ aawe anɔmi anyɔ mafuĩdza-ɔ mapia i itɔkpe nɛ loisɛ inyi ame. Yesu Ɔte Ara I Sɛrɛrerɔ̃ Gɔ Loyu Iso.
translated: If you believe in your heart and confess with your mouth, you will be saved! Just as the mouth speaks what the heart believes, so also the mouth confesses what the heart trusts and is saved. Jesus is the only way to God.

ref: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
text: “Nto loledza mi iɣere sɔ ira biara nɛ i mi maturi inyɔ aatɔrã misia ita i kayi gagbe iso i kayi ikparama ame ne, wũ Ɔse gɔ i kato to ɔ̃abara ne ɔtã mi.
translated: If you see someone who is in trouble and you have the ability to help but you refuse, God will judge you together with that person.

ref: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
text: Gɔ ɔɖabo gɔmɔ na sikã ɔ̃atã kuso ɔso ne, igara ɔtã sɔ marɔdɛ̃ ɔ̃ gu ɔ̃ ɔre gu mabi lɛ masande gu ira biara nɛ ɔba si masu matã kuso.
translated: If someone wants to buy something from the market, they should first get money and then go to the market to buy whatever they want.

ref: When some other officials found out what had happened, they felt sorry for the man who had been put in jail. Then they told the king what had happened.
text: Gɔ ɔ̃ maɖabo laa ɔnya nnɛ loba ne, ibua iɖaa ma, ne masɛ maaɣere igara ira biara nɛ loba ne.
translated: If you want to do something good today, start now, and don't wait until tomorrow to do that good thing.

ref: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
text: “Ne ɔso igara ɔpia makpere ɔɖabo mabɔ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, ‘Ɔɖabo lalaa aɖe! Losu fɔ kuso gɔ ato me ɔɖuɖu lotsɛ-ɔ alasɔ atã me iti.
translated: He took the basket and said to the child, ‘Child, come here! Take this and put it on the table in the middle of the room.’

ref: When Jesus finished teaching, he left Galilee and went to the part of Judea east of the Jordan River.
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔɣɛ ara wagbe ɔro ne, ɔrui i Galilea ɔkɛlɛ Yudea awo ame i Yordan Ɔwore kama isɛ.
translated: When Jesus heard that he had died, he left Galilee and Judea and went to the people by the Jordan River.

ref: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
text: Ne ɔso maisiɖe maturi inyɔ ɣɛɛ ɔwɛ̃. Ne ɔso kuwɛ̃ si ɔdaabɛse nnɛ i Ɣaa ɔsu ɔmatãra so iso.”
translated: If you plant maize early, it will do well. If you plant it late and the rains stop, you will not get a good harvest.

ref: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
text: Ne ɔɣere ma ma wũ sɔ, ‘Mikɛlɛ wũ kakpɛkɔ̃ miabara karabara ne nto loatã mi ikpɛnɛ kuso.’
translated: And the woman said, ‘Michael, you should not go to the farm today because there are many snakes there.’

ref: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
text: ‘Mma lotsɔra sia ɔba ne, ɔɖɔwũ ɔwɛ̃ pɛ mabara karabara atoa sɔ bo ne, bobara karabara ikpɛnɛ ɔɖuɖu i kuɣɛ̃ ame, ɣɛɛ kuso kuwɛ̃ ɔtã bo!’
translated: This woman is not here, she has gone to the farm early in the morning, if you want to see her, wait until she returns!

ref: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
text: Ma ara ɔɖo Yesu kayiri, ne ɔpɛgu ma anɔmi matsɛ inya fiɛ marui masiai wũ ne.
translated: If I follow Jesus truly, then I will not be afraid because I know he is always with me.

ref: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.
text: Zebedeo ɔre ɔkɔ ɔ̃ mabi inyɔ maba Yesu kɔrɛ, mapɛ agɛgɛ̃ makarɛ wũ sɔ ɔbuai ma.
translated: Zebedee’s son is going with his mother to where Jesus is, to ask him for something important.

ref: But the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were angry when they saw his miracles and heard the children shouting praises to the Son of David.
text: Ɣɛɛ gɔ masɔrɛdze makpakpa gu mmara matedze ɔnya awawãra wa ɔto ɔbara gu kumɛgɔ i mabiɛtɛ to mala kulu i isɔrɛyo ame sɔ, “Maale David Ɔbi!” ne, ikpɛ̃ ma sikpã.
translated: As they were leaving Jericho, a large crowd followed him. Two blind men were sitting by the roadside, and when they heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, “Lord, Son of David!” and asked for mercy.

ref: Then Jesus left the city and went out to the village of Bethany, where he spent the night. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Mark
text: Ne kama ne, Yesu ɔrui ɔnyua ma ɔkɛlɛ Betania ɔmagɛ̃ ame ngbegɔ ɔsɛ ɔ̃arɛ. Yesu Ɔgbarĩ Kuɖedziri Kuwɛ̃.
translated: Six days before the Passover, Jesus came to Bethany where Lazarus was, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.

ref: But Jesus said to them, “If you have faith and don't doubt, I promise you can do what I did to this tree. And you will be able to do even more. You can tell this mountain to get up and jump into the sea, and it will.
text: Ne Yesu ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Nto loɣere mi gbaã sɔ si mifɔ miɖe fiɛ miiwararã ne, mito miawo nnɛ lobara kudziri gɔgbe ɔbara. Fiɛ iiɖe ne kere, ɣɛɛ mito miaɣere kube gɔgbe sɔ, ‘Kɛlɛ aafuĩdza so apia i ɔpo ame,’ fiɛ iaba ngbɔ.
translated: Jesus often told his followers, “Truly I tell you, if you have faith as small as a mustard seed, you can say to this mountain, ‘Move from here to there,’ and it will move. Nothing will be impossible for you.”

ref: Jesus told the chief priests and leaders to listen to this story: A land owner once planted a vineyard. He built a wall around it and dug a pit to crush the grapes in. He also built a lookout tower. Then he rented out his vineyard and left the country.
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Minɔ igbã mama ana. Kpɛ̃dze ɔwɛ̃ ɔwe ku ɔwi gɔ lofe gɔ ɔkpɛ̃ adziribi wa masɛ masu mabara ala ndã kakpɛkɔ̃. Ɔtɛ̃ ɔkpã ɔki ɔlɔ kakpɛkɔ̃, ɔɖi itawo nɛ ame maakã ndã, fiɛ ka ɔtsue iyo kɔlɛa ngbegɔ i ngɔ loadzuɛ̃ kakpɛkɔ̃ aaya. Ɔsu kakpɛkɔ̃ ɔpia i marabaradze nrɔɔ̃ ame fiɛ ɔkɛlɛ ɔri.
translated: Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you. Unless a grain of wheat falls to the ground and dies, it remains only a single seed. But if it dies, it produces many seeds. Anyone who loves their life will lose it, while anyone who hates their life in this world will keep it for eternal life.

ref: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
text: Alasɔ Ndupiedze Yohanes ɔba ɔ̃ate mi sibɔlɔlɔ ɔri, ɣɛɛ miifɔ wũ miɖe, ɣɛɛ ma lampofɔdze gu asɔrɔ̃maɖedze ɔfɔ wũ maɖe. Atoa sɔ minya nwagbe ne, miifinikira mi adzuni mifɔ wũ miɖe.” Yesu Ɔpɛ Igbã I Marabaradze Lalaa Mawɛ̃ Iso.
translated: After John’s messengers left, Jesus began to speak to the crowd about John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed swayed by the wind? If not, what did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? No, those who wear expensive clothes and indulge in luxury are in palaces. But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.”

ref: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
text: Matã mmuai sɔ, “Ɔto ɔ̃aɖoe marabaradze lalaa magbe ɔsu kakpɛkɔ̃ ɔtã marabaradze mama ma loasu ɔ̃ kaɖekɔ̃ mabɔ wũ i ɔwi iso si masɔrɛ̃ ɔraɖui.”
translated: "The elder said, 'If you want to walk the long journey, do not start with haste, but rather with patience, so that you may reach your destination.'"

ref: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
text: Ne Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Miika i Ɔko Sekelea ame sɔ, ‘Ita nɛ i mayotsuedze ɔsɛ̃ sɔ iilɛ ne, ne loba iakpese ita nɛ lofɔ kuɣa iɖo ata ɔɖuɖu pelepele ne. Bosate lobara ira siare nɛgbe fiɛ ibo bo anɔ gbaã!’ ”
translated: But Jesus said, "Someone from the crowd said, 'Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.' But he replied to him, 'Man, who appointed me a judge or arbiter between you?'"

ref: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
text: Ma Farisise ɔsɛ maabara adzuni sɔ maamɔɛ̃ Yesu i ma atɔ̃me ikarɛ ame.
translated: Some Pharisees who loved money were listening to all this and sneering at Jesus.

ref: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
text: Ɔpia ɔ̃ maɖabo sɔ masɛ maakpere mma matã itsue sɔ maba iyi kaɖekɔ̃ sɔ ka maba, ɣɛɛ masɛ̃ sɔ maito maba.
translated: If I go and I do not meet him, I will return and tell you that I did not see him, but if I meet him, I will bring him to you.

ref: Go out to the street corners and tell everyone you meet to come to the banquet.”
text: Ne ɔso mikɛlɛ siri iso si mikpere ɔbiara gɔ mianya mibɔ.’
translated: But Michael said that he would not give anything to anyone.

ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mmɔ miɣɛrɛ ne, alasɔ miiɣe Ɔko Sekelea ame atɔ̃me fiɛ miiɣe Ɣaa ɔle ana.
translated: Jesus answered them, “Truly I tell you, unless someone is born of water and the Spirit, he cannot enter the kingdom of God.

ref: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
text: Ɔkarɛ wũ sɔ, “Ratedze, mme mmara lomɔ miɖo?”
translated: "The man said, 'Teacher, what must I do?'"

ref: This is the first and most important commandment.
text: Mmɛgbe nɖe mmara ɖeakatɔ̃ gu mmɛ lonya miɖo pelepele ne.
translated: When you hear the law, let it enter your heart and guide your actions.

ref: Jesus replied, “How then could the Spirit lead David to call the Messiah his Lord? David said,
text: Ne Yesu ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Si igara David ɔbi ɔɖe ne, lɛ ise ɔso i David ɔki i Ɣaa Siwarã ɔle ame ɔkpere wũ sɔ Bosate? David ɔɣɛ sɔ,
translated: Jesus said to them, "How can they say that the Christ is David’s son? For David himself says in the Book of Psalms,"

ref: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
text: Si igara David ɔkpere wũ Bosate ne, nda i Kristo aaɖe ɔ̃ ɔbi ɔɣɛ?”
translated: If David calls him Lord, how then can he be his son?

ref: Anyone who swears by the altar also swears by everything on it.
text: Ne ɔso si ɔturi ɔtarĩgu isɔrɛkasege ne, ta ara wa nsia i isɔrɛkasege iso ɔɖuɖu iɖe ɔtarĩgu.
translated: If someone puts a pot on the fire and does not put water in the pot, the pot will burn on the fire.

ref: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
text: Si makpere ma i ɔpɔrɔ̃ katukɔ̃ ne, masɛ maɖɔɛ kusɛkɔ̃ kɔlɛa. Masɛ maɖɔɛ masɛ i kusɛkɔ̃ sɛɛ gu kusɛkɔ̃ kɔlɛa i ma kusarɛkɔ̃ ana.
translated: If you want to eat a ripe orange, you must first peel it. You must first peel it because the peel is not as sweet as the inside.

ref: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
text: “Madaakpere mi sɔ ratedze, alasɔ mi ɔɖuɖu ne, manyii miɖe fiɛ ratedze ɔwɛ̃ saã miba.
translated: If you see my footprints, know that I have passed by, if you do not see my footprints, then I have not passed by.

ref: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
text: “Ndzo miɖe mitã mi ma nto mikɔ maturi ɣɛɛ manɔbiɛdze miɖe! Misɛ mite sɔ, ‘Si ɔturi ɔtarĩgu Ɣaa isɔrɛyo ne, mmara iinyi wũ, ɣɛɛ si ɔtarĩgu sikã dzɛ nse i isɔrɛyo ne, ɔ̃ i mmara ɔnyi ne.’
translated: So I tell you all, do not be like the foolish girls! Be like the ones who, when the bridegroom was delayed, took oil with them, and not like those who did not take oil with them.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
text: “Ndzo miɖe mitã mi mmara matedze gu ma Farisise! Mi ma nsɛ mibara ara sɔ si mibo maturi anɔ! Mise lɛ maturi akpibiɔ wa matsue makua kukaakɔ, ɣɛɛ wã ame ɔyi ku makpise buria ku akui.
translated: Don’t think that you are better than the Pharisees! You too do things that you know are not right! You say the right things with your mouths but your hearts are far away, and you do not love your fellow human beings or help those in need.

ref: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔbɔrɛ i Ɣaa isɔrɛyo ɔto ɔkɛlɛ ne, ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔba maakarɛ wũ si ɔkarã inɔ i kumɛgɔ matsue ne ayo iso.
translated: When Jesus was walking by the Sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers who were fishermen casting a net into the lake.

ref: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark

translated: When the devil wants to catch someone, he first makes the person proud, then he catches him with pride.

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: Ɣɛɛ ne, imusuora biara nɛ i ɔturi aaɣɛ i Siwarã Bielea iso ne, Ɣaa iibasu ɔtsɛ wũ kuiyikuiyi. Maibasu, aɣɛrɛ wagbe matsɛ manya.”
translated: So, every morning when the elders gather at the Siwarã Bielea, they discuss important matters. After the discussions, everyone goes back to their homes.

ref: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: Ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Kpɛ̃dze ɔwɛ̃ ɔsɛ ɔraɖui kasakɔ̃.
translated: The woman said, "Bring that thing so I can see it clearly."

ref: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
text: Matsɛrɛ masɛ i Ɔko Sekelea ame sɔ, ‘Mato maanya, ɣɛɛ maibanya maɖi ite. Mato maanɔ, ɣɛɛ maibanɔ karɔ̃, sɔ si maibafiniki fiɛ Ɣaa aasu ma akpi ɔtsɛ ma.’ ”
translated: The elders told Ɔko Sekelea, ‘If you go, you will see what you are looking for. If you do not go, you will not find it, because what you are searching for is not going to come and meet you at home.’ ”

ref: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.
text: Maturi mawɛ̃ se lɛ ɔraɖui gɔ loɣo i ɔri kuruɛ. Gɔ manɔ ne Ɔbosam ɔmɔɛ̃ ɔɖi i ma iti ame ɔluwɛ̃.
translated: Children like to play in the sand when the sun is hot. At night the moon comes out and everyone goes to sleep.

ref: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
text: Ɔraɖui sadze gɔgbe to ɔsa Ɣaa itɔ̃me.
translated: The hunter set a trap in the forest last night.

ref: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
text: Si abi ɔrɛtɛ ne, kpɛ̃dze to ɔ̃asɔrɛ̃, alasɔ ɔraɖui ɔsɔrɛ̃wi ɔɔɣo.” Igbã Isegu Idziribi Kosoi Iso.
translated: If you do good, even if you wake up early, the one who does evil wakes up earlier.

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: Ala ɔɣere so sɔ, “Si loana ɔ̃ awu kere lopɛgu ne, loto loasarɛ.”
translated: "The woman said, 'If you want to eat this yam, you must ask for it first.'"

ref: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
text: Ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔgbe kawekɔ̃ nɖe maturi kabiarakɔ̃. Kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔwo wũ ɔnyi ku ala nane ngɔsɔngɔsɔn.
translated: The old man went to the farm early in the morning. When he arrived, he saw that the yams had been eaten by animals.

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
text: Ɔwi gbodzoo si mawo wũ nrɔɔ̃ gu ngba ɔnyi ne, ɔsɛ ɔtu ngɔsɔngɔsɔn gu kugbagɔrɔĩ ɔɖi so. Ɔwe ɔle gɔ kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔwo wũ ɔmɔɛ̃.
translated: The hunter hides traps in the forest so that when an animal steps on them, it gets caught. He does this because he wants to get meat for his family.

ref: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Makparako gbodzoo ɣɛ mato maɖe ara i kabemi kawɛ̃ kuruɛ i mmɔ.
translated: Makparako is very hungry but there is nothing for him to eat except the little yam his mother left.

ref: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
text: Ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ gɔ marɔ Yairo fiɛ ɔɖe ma Yudase kasarɛkɔ̃ ɔkpakpa ɔba mmɔ. Gɔ ɔnya Yesu ne, ɔpɛ agɛgɛ̃ i ɔ̃ katɔ̃.
translated: While he was still speaking, someone from the house of Jairus, the synagogue leader, came and said, “Your daughter is dead. Don’t bother the teacher anymore.”

ref: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
text: Ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Nyɔ kumɛgɔ i maturi ɔki malɔ bo. Be ɔso fiɛ ato akarɛ sɔ nna lopɛgu-ɔ?”
translated: He looked at her and said, “You know I cannot go with you. But who will take care of the children if I am not here?”

ref: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
text: Yesu ɣɛ ɔto ɔpiapia anɔmi sɔ ɔ̃anya ngɔ lopɛgu wũ.
translated: Jesus took the bread and gave thanks before he broke it.

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
text: Ɔrɔ̃go ana ɔtsue kumɛgɔ ka ise wũ. Ne ɔso i ifɔ̃ ame ne, ɔsɛ ɔba ɔ̃apɛ agɛgɛ̃ i Yesu ngbatɔ̃. Ne ɔɖi ɔte wũ kumɛgɔ iba ne.
translated: The man was very sick and lay down. When his family saw him, they called the name of Jesus for help. After that, he got up from his sickness.

ref: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
text: Yesu iifɔ itɔ̃me ne maɣɛ mmɔ. Ne ɔɣere Yairo sɔ, “Daanigã, fɔɖe kere!”
translated: Jesus went with them. He said to Jairus, "Little girl, get up!"

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Gɔ mawara itubu ne, maba maabo Genezaret karɔ̃ iso, ne ɔso mapɛ ma ɔkolo ikpo i ibu katɔ̃.
translated: One day, Jesus was standing by the Lake of Gennesaret, and the people were crowding around him and listening to the word of God.

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Ɣaa kanyaɖidze ba kuwarɛ i kakɔi biara iɖo ɔ̃ mɔmɔ ɔmagɛ̃, ɔ̃ maturi gu ɔ̃ iyo maturi anɔmi ame.”
translated: Jesus answered them, “Foxes have holes and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head.”

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: Ɣɛɛ kakɔi biara ga maturi loifɔ mi ɣee maikã mi atsue ne, mikpakpa mi ngba iso sise mifere lɛ Ɣaa kutsue ikpadzɛ̃ nɛ loaba ikparĩ.”
translated: Even if you have many friends, if you do not have wisdom and patience, your friends will leave you and you will be left alone.

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
text: Ne ɔso, ɔbitɛ gɔgbe ɔsɛ ɔ̃akarɛ ɔ̃ ɔnyi sɔ, “Be si lokarɛ?” Ne ɔ̃ ɔnyi ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Karɛ wũ Ndupiedze Yohanes iti.”
translated: At night, he came and asked him, “Who did you see?” And he replied, “I saw John the Baptist.”

ref: The guard put the head on a serving plate and took it to the girl. Then she gave it to her mother.
text: Ɔsu ne ɔsia i kategelei iso, ɔsu ɔba ɔ̃atã ɔbitɛ gɔgbe. Ne ɔfɔ, ɔsu ɔkɛlɛgu ɔ̃ ɔnyi ne.
translated: Rain and sun do not fall together, when rain comes, the sun goes away. Therefore, rain does not meet the sun.

ref: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
text: Gɔ kutsuɛ ɔɣo ne, marasuãdze ɔbore mabo wũ maɣere wũ sɔ, “Kaɖe ɔɔro fiɛ ngbe ɔdzorogu ayo ame.
translated: He took the knife and, after sharpening it well, said, “Now this knife will not be blunt for anyone.”

ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mi mɔmɔ mitã ma irere maɖe.” Ne maɣere wũ sɔ, “Nnɛmɔ to iafɔ lɛ ɔturi awa farafana kuso. Ato abie sɔ bonina ngbɔ itã ma aɖera?”
translated: Jesus answered him, “Truly I tell you, today you will be with me in paradise.” But he replied, “Are you not the Christ? Save yourself and us.”

ref: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Manigã gbaã sɔ manya wũ i ndu iso. Ne Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Midaanigã! Mme loɖe. Mina ɔtu.”
translated: That person was lying down on his mat. When Jesus saw him, he said, “My friend! Get up. Take your mat.”

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ Yesu ɔtã ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔya mabo i ɔkolo ame sɔ mawara makɛlɛ Betsaida. Ne ɔ̃ wũ ka ɔɖi iturikuri ɔri ne.
translated: One day Jesus took a blind man by the hand and led him out of the village of Bethsaida. When he had spit on the man's eyes, he put his hands on him.

ref: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
text: Si kutsuɛ aaɣo ne, ɔkolo ka ɔsɛ i ibu ndɛ̃, Yesu ɔnɔwɛ̃ ana ɣɛ i ibu katɔ̃.
translated: If you believe so, the elder says to his child, Jesus also loves his children.

ref: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
text: Ne ɔya ɔbo i ɔkolo ame fiɛ ɔwiri ɔnyua ikpɛ̃ ne. Ibara ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɣaa gbaã!
translated: If someone throws a stone at you, don't throw it back. Instead, walk away in peace!

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Ne ɔso ngɔi mɛ pelepele i Yesu ɔki, simagɛ̃ ame o-o, nsɛkɔ̃ o-o ne, maturi ɔsore manyɛse mabɔ kuɣakaɖekɔ̃ fiɛ mapɛ wũ nrɔɔ̃ sɔ ɔtã mapɛgu bidɛ ɔ̃ awu kutsueti kere. Mma ɔɖuɖu lopɛgu wũ ne, mana isosare.
translated: If someone believes in Jesus, they will not die, they will not perish, but will have eternal life and will live forever with Him. Even if they die here, they will rise again.
